Details: Ultraviolet (UV) irradiation combined with titanium dioxide (TiO2) as a photocatalyst was shown to be effective for mevinphos removal. One study investigated degradation of mevinphos (based on COD reduction) at two pH levels (6.5 and 9.0) using a UV dose of 3 mW/cm2 and 1 g/L TiO2 [1654]. The results showed 77.2 and 85.4 percent of COD reduction at pH 6.5 and 9.0, respectively, with an approximate UV contact time of three hours [1654]. UV alone is not effective for mevinphos removal; only 30.4 percent COD reduction was achieved at pH 9.0 with an approximate UV contact time of three hours [1654].
